# WA diesel stockpile being considered by government as nation grapples with fuel worries

- [By Callum Liddelow](https://www.abc.net.au/news/callum-liddelow/106235996)
- [Topic:Road Transport Industry](https://www.abc.net.au/news/topic/road-transport-industry)

20h ago20 hours agoSat 11 Apr 2026 at 11:52pm

![A hand on a diesel pump at a petrol station.](https://live-production.wcms.abc-cdn.net.au/3fb1c5da15d03715a51ee2cf52eb6033?impolicy=wcms_crop_resize&cropH=2270&cropW=4036&xPos=0&yPos=114&width=862&height=485)

The WA government is considering stockpiling its own diesel in the event of any future fuel shocks.  (ABC News: Craig Heerey )

## In short:

The WA government says it is considering creating a stockpile of diesel to insulate the state from any future fuel shocks, such as the one brought on by the Iran war.

WA Energy Minister Amber-Jade Sanderson said the reserve would be "millions of litres" in size, but could not give specifics on exactly how large.

She said the state had been working with the mining industry to identify spare storage capacity for the stockpile.

abc.net.au/news/fuel-worries-prompt-wa-government-to-consider-diesel-stockpile/106555882

Link copied

ShareShare article

The WA government is considering creating its own "strategic stockpile" of diesel, saying the state's size and the reliance of the agriculture and resources industries on the fuel warrants a separate reserve.

The federal governmental already requires fuel retailers to hold a minimum of around four weeks reserve of both petrol and diesel, to be released at the Commonwealth's discretion to shore up supply.

But WA Energy Minister Amber-Jade Sanderson said this system had not been working optimally for her state as the war in Iran put pressure on stocks across the nation.

"What we found with the \[minimum\] stock obligation is that not every fuel supplier had their store in Western Australia," Ms Sanderson said.

![Amber-Jade Sanderson stands speaking at a press conference. ](https://live-production.wcms.abc-cdn.net.au/ad71c64592e390c2696eac952b918ba2?impolicy=wcms_crop_resize&cropH=1080&cropW=1620&xPos=150&yPos=0&width=862&height=575)

Amber-Jade Sanderson says high-need areas, such as the agricultural sector, would be prioritised with the stockpile.  (ABC News)

"We had the majority with BP, they had their their storage in Western Australia, but other operators, Viva and Ampol for example, they do not keep their stock in Western Australia — it's in Queensland."

The minister said the stockpile would be "millions of litres" in size.

"I expect as those discussions come to a conclusion we'll be able to be transparent about those volumes, but we're in discussions currently at the moment," she said.

She said the state had been working with the mining industry to identify spare storage capacity for the stockpile.

## 'Extra resilience'

The fuel transport network already has the capacity to distribute the additional diesel, Ms Sanderson said, and smaller distribution operators like independent truck drivers would become key to servicing the Wheatbelt and Goldfields regions.

[**Remote communities vulnerable to global fuel crisis**](https://www.abc.net.au/news/2026-03-24/remote-communities-australia-powered-diesel-left-vulnerable-/106464204)

![An aerial photo of a remote desert community at the edge of an ephemeral salt lake.](https://live-production.wcms.abc-cdn.net.au/83c673ef97cbdadc378264f90f00055c?impolicy=wcms_crop_resize&cropH=3078&cropW=5472&xPos=0&yPos=285&width=862&height=485)

Remote communities' reliance on diesel fuel for power leaves them vulnerable in a global crisis.

"The first point of failure of the supply chain was the independent spot market, that a lot of those agricultural operators and smaller mining operators rely on," she said.

"\[A diesel stockpile\] would allow us essentially to release fuel onto that spot market as required, if we see another spike in demand and that spot market evaporates.

"It will also provide extra resilience in regional and remote areas, in particular, our remote communities.

"This would be solely for West Australians and be directed at the discretion of the state government to areas that need it most, for example agricultural areas at the end of those supply chains, or remote communities that still rely significantly on diesel."

Ms Sanderson said she did not expect a separate stockpile to cause tensions with the federal government.

![A sign outside a Vibe service station showing the price of unleaded petrol, discount unleaded petrol and diesel.](https://live-production.wcms.abc-cdn.net.au/ae25faeea1aeeb6714c7cf1391d0bb1d?impolicy=wcms_crop_resize&cropH=1080&cropW=1620&xPos=167&yPos=0&width=862&height=575)

The price of fuel across the nation spiked after the outbreak of war in the Middle East.  (ABC News)

"It is a requirement for states to receive their fair share \[of the federal fuel reserve\]. What we have done as a state is move quicker than any other state to establish the architecture we need to understand the supply chain, but also be able to move the fuel from A to B where there are complex situations," she said.

There are no plans yet for a stockpile of petrol.

> "The priority is diesel. The most challenging part, particularly for remote communities, for the agricultural sector and the resources sector, is their high use of diesel,"

Ms Sanderson said.

## 'Glaring void'

WA Opposition Leader Basil Zempilas referred to the government's inability to prepare for fuel shortages as a "glaring void".

"Successive energy ministers have dropped the ball on future planning in this space," Mr Zempilas said.

![Basil Zempilas speaks at a press conference with a serious expression.](https://live-production.wcms.abc-cdn.net.au/4f3ef566898b59f5ff5961a963792efd?impolicy=wcms_crop_resize&cropH=1080&cropW=1620&xPos=240&yPos=0&width=862&height=575)

Basil Zempilas says the government needed to act sooner in stockpiling its own fuel.  (ABC News)

"I'm pleased to see the government turning their attention to it," Mr Zempilas said, before noting there was "very little detail" in the proposal.

"As we said six weeks ago, we need our own reserves and we need to be able to establish a way in which Western Australians … aren't compromised when we have a global event like this in the future."

## Warning to petrol retailers

Ms Sanderson also had a message for fuel operators she said were "continuing to rip off customers despite the reduction in the fuel excise".

"The state government is working closely with the ACCC to monitor the margins between the wholesale price and the retail price for both unleaded and diesel fuels," she said.

![Hoses at a fuel bowser, some of which say "Sorry this hose not in use."](https://live-production.wcms.abc-cdn.net.au/c62d53db5c33cb84ffb4c93ef50b5eda?impolicy=wcms_crop_resize&cropH=2813&cropW=4220&xPos=390&yPos=0&width=862&height=575)

The state government is moving to shore up WA's diesel supplies.  (ABC News: Andrew Chounding)

"We do expect retailers to pass on that \[reduction\] on."

She said the commissioner for consumer protection was investigating retailers, who would take action if any breaches of consumer law were found.

Ms Sanderson said prices were "tracking downwards" since the reduction in the excise.

metadata.ogDescription: The WA government is considering creating its own "strategic stockpile" of diesel, saying the state's size and the reliance of the agriculture and resources industries on the fuel warrants a separate reserve.
metadata.og:description: The WA government is considering creating its own "strategic stockpile" of diesel, saying the state's size and the reliance of the agriculture and resources industries on the fuel warrants a separate reserve.
metadata.keywords: perth news, wa news, fuel prices, fuel shortages, wa fuel supply, australia's fuel supply, how much fuel does wa have, amber jade sanderson
metadata.ogTitle: WA considering separate stockpile of diesel amid fuel shortages
metadata.title: WA diesel stockpile being considered by government as nation grapples with fuel worries - ABC News
metadata.twitter:image:alt: A hand on a diesel pump at a petrol station.
article:tag: how much fuel does wa have

metadata.og:title: WA considering separate stockpile of diesel amid fuel shortages
metadata.description: The WA government is considering creating its own "strategic stockpile" of diesel, saying the state's size and the reliance of the agriculture and resources industries on the fuel warrants a separate reserve.
